queue.task: Session B3: Review TODO items and earlier session findings. Plan evening depth work — BUILD ceiling at 58/60 so EXPLORE/MAINTAIN focus.
queue.summary: Evening review: BUILD ceiling 58/60 confirmed. Session is EXPLORE/MAINTAIN only. Queue has db.js monolith analysis and WAL truncation deep dives — good architectural depth work.

queue.task: Deep dive: HenryDB db.js monolith analysis — map the 7K+ line file structure, identify module boundaries for splitting
queue.summary: db.js monolith: 9844 lines, 142 methods, single Database class. Mapped 8 extraction domains. Biggest wins: ExprEval (1154 LOC), Joins+Optimizer (1390), SELECT (1341). Found duplicate _analyzeTable (L1807 dead code shadowed by L5654). Wrote scratch note with full analysis and extraction order.

queue.task: HenryDB: WAL truncation gap analysis — understand current checkpoint behavior, measure WAL growth
queue.summary: WAL truncation analysis complete. TransactionalDB works correctly (checkpoint + ftruncate + auto at 16MB). PersistentDB is the gap — uses FileWAL but never calls checkpoint/truncate, so WAL grows forever. WALManager non-inMemory truncate() is a no-op. Wrote scratch note with full analysis.

queue.task: Final session push, knowledge capture, CURRENT.md update
queue.summary: Git push, CURRENT.md updated, 4 scratch notes indexed, daily log appended.

queue.task: HenryDB: MVCC interception analysis — map how MVCC visibility is threaded through SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
queue.summary: MVCC interception analysis: heap.scan/delete/findByPK/get monkey-patched in _installScanInterceptors(). Version maps track xmin/xmax per row. Fragile (5 risk factors identified) but pragmatic. Key insight: findByPK falls back to full scan when B+tree version not visible. Wrote scratch note.

queue.task: HenryDB: Compiled query engine analysis — where does it diverge from interpreter? Map the coverage gap.
queue.summary: Compiled engine analysis: 4041 LOC, only handles 4 expr types (COMPARE/AND/OR/NOT). Silent null filter → correctness bug (latent, only EXPLAIN COMPILED uses it, not main query path). Vectorized engine is better path. AdaptiveEngine also not wired in. Wrote scratch note.

queue.task: HenryDB: Test suite health — what's the actual pass/fail/timeout breakdown right now?
queue.summary: Test health sampling: 847 test files, 8642 test cases. Compiled/vectorized/adaptive: 50/50 pass. Core infra (MVCC+SSI+WAL+crash-recovery): 48/49 (1 flaky WAL perf test, passed on retry). Healthy codebase.

queue.task: HenryDB: LOC census — how big is this project really? Count by category.
queue.summary: LOC census: 200,229 total (75,137 source + 125,092 tests across 1,177 files). db.js monolith is 9,844 LOC. 330 source files, 847 test files, 8,642 individual test cases. Uncategorized 38K LOC includes PL/SQL, Raft, partitioning, RBAC, jsonpath, and more.

queue.task: Review project portfolio: what's the state of monkey-lang, ray-tracer, neural-net? Set tomorrow's direction.
queue.summary: Portfolio review: HenryDB dominated today (200K LOC). Deep architecture analysis produced 4 scratch notes. Tomorrow: HenryDB fix day (hash join wiring, compiled engine null bug, WAL truncation). Consider monkey-lang for variety.

queue.task: HenryDB: Read and audit the parser (sql.js 3206 LOC) — what SQL standard features are supported vs missing?
queue.summary: Parser audit: sql.js handles 82+ SQL features including GRANT/REVOKE, UPSERT, PARTITION BY, window frames, GROUPING SETS, CALL. Missing from parser: JSON_TABLE, XML, EXCLUSION constraints. Cursors/enums/domains exist as standalone modules but not integrated into main engine.

queue.task: HenryDB: Volcano iterator model audit — what operators exist and how complete is the physical plan?
queue.summary: Volcano model: 17 operators (1129 LOC). 11 wired into planner (SeqScan, Filter, Project, Limit, Sort, Distinct, HashJoin, NestedLoopJoin, IndexNestedLoopJoin, HashAggregate, ValuesIter). 6 defined but unwired (Union, Window, CTE, RecursiveCTE, MergeJoin, IndexScan). These features use db.js interpreter path instead.

queue.task: HenryDB: pg-server wire protocol — test against a real PostgreSQL client (psql or pg npm module)
queue.summary: PG wire protocol: 26/26 tests pass (pg-server + pg-client). Server handles auth (trust + MD5 + SCRAM), query protocol, extended query protocol. psql not installed locally but test suite validates thoroughly via pg npm module.

queue.task: HenryDB: Planner cost model accuracy — run some test queries and compare estimated vs actual row counts
queue.summary: Cost model accuracy: After ANALYZE, histogram-based estimates are excellent (0-24% error, avg ~7%). BUT planTree estimates use generic defaults (1/3 for range, 1/10 for equality) — two cost models running in parallel. The analysis path uses real histogram stats; the planner tree uses fallback constants. This divergence means the planner may make suboptimal join/scan decisions despite good stats.

queue.task: HenryDB: Stress test MVCC concurrency — can we trigger write-write conflicts and verify isolation?
queue.summary: MVCC concurrency stress: snapshot isolation ✅, phantom prevention ✅, dirty read prevention ✅. BUT write-write conflict detection ❌ (no error raised when two txns update same row). Lost update confirmed: T1 commits 1100, T2 commits 1200, final=1100 (T2 silently lost). Real concurrency bug — either conflict detection or last-writer-wins is broken.

queue.task: HenryDB: How does SSI (Serializable Snapshot Isolation) differ from regular MVCC? Does it catch the lost update?
queue.summary: SSI mode also fails to catch lost update — same bug. Root cause: delete interceptor only checks activeTxns for write-write conflict, but committed txns are already removed. Need to check if row was modified after snapshot start (like PostgreSQL first-updater-wins). Both snapshot and serializable isolation levels have this bug.

queue.task: HenryDB: Investigate the lost update root cause deeper — exactly what happens in the version map during concurrent UPDATE
queue.summary: Root cause identified: T2 UPDATE returns 0 rows affected (silently does nothing). T2 MVCC scan sees old row for SELECT but UPDATE path fails to locate/modify it after T1 committed. The lost update is actually a silent no-op UPDATE, not overwrite. T1 value preserved but T2 thinks it updated when it didnt. PostgreSQL would either block (wait for T1 commit) or error with could not serialize access.

queue.task: HenryDB: What happens with 3+ concurrent transactions? Test phantom write skew.
queue.summary: SSI correctly catches write skew (doctor on-call pattern)! T2 commit rejected with Serialization failure: dangerous structure detected. So SSI works for different-row conflicts but not same-row updates (lost update bug is specific to concurrent UPDATE of same row).

queue.task: HenryDB: Test concurrent INSERT + DELETE isolation behaviors
queue.summary: Concurrent DELETE: ✅ write-write conflict correctly detected (throws error). Concurrent INSERT isolation: ✅ uncommitted inserts invisible. Key insight: DELETE conflict detection WORKS (both txns target same physical row), but UPDATE fails because T1 commit physicalizes the delete (removes row from heap), so T2 UPDATE scans a different set of rows → 0 rows affected. The bug is specifically in UPDATE, not DELETE.

queue.task: HenryDB: Narrow the UPDATE bug — is it _physicalizeDeletes or the UPDATE scan path?
queue.summary: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: UPDATE uses B+tree index scan for WHERE id=X. The PK index points to T1s NEW row (0:1, xmin=2) which is invisible to T2s snapshot. heap.get() returns null for invisible row. usedIndex=true, so fallback full scan is SKIPPED. Fix: when index scan finds 0 visible rows, fall through to full table scan. The bug is specifically in the index-scan optimization path of _update, not in MVCC itself.
